summary: Iteration 215 — Validated the extraction of all per-lead proposal endpoints from server.py into routes/lead_proposals.py and confirmed the 4 new modern brand fonts (Poppins, Montserrat, Lato, Roboto Slab) are wired end-to-end. Backend: 10/10 pytest pass (3.6s). Verified: GET /api/leads/{id}/proposal (200), GET .../proposal/customization (returns template+templates+template_id+company_name+override), POST .../proposal/preview (returns %PDF- bytes, content-type application/pdf), PUT .../proposal/customization (has_override=true) then DELETE (ok, has_override cleared), POST .../proposal/generate (creates a new version: v28→v29 confirmed; message='Proposal v{n} generated successfully'; content_type=application/pdf), POST .../proposal/share-email (correctly rejected non-approved with 400, proving routing). Modern-font embedding: for each of poppins/montserrat/lato/robotoslab, created a throwaway template via POST /api/proposals/templates, applied font_key everywhere via PUT /api/proposals/templates/{id}, hit /proposal/preview with template_id, and asserted the chosen TTF (Poppins/Montserrat/Lato/RobotoSlab regular OR bold) appears in /BaseFont entries of the generated PDF — all 4 passed. Throwaway templates cleaned up. Frontend: logged in as CEO, opened lead /leads/08c93122-… ; Proposal section in right-hand rail shows version 28 'Pending Review' with View PDF/Download/Customize & Regenerate/Replace Proposal/Approve/Request Changes/Reject — no errors. Clicked Customize & Regenerate → dialog opened, Template selector preloaded 'Default · default', sections list (#1 Paragraph, #2 Pricing table, etc.) rendered, 'Update preview' clickable. Visited /proposal-template, opened Title font combobox inside data-testid='tpl-title-card' → all 8 options visible in correct order: DejaVu Sans (₹ support), Helvetica, Times, Courier, Poppins (modern), Montserrat (modern), Lato (modern), Roboto Slab (serif). Selected 'Poppins (modern)', clicked save-template-btn → toast 'Proposal template saved' confirmed persistence (API GET later showed title.font='poppins'). Restored title.font to 'dejavu' via PUT (template not left dirty) and a final POST /generate produced v29 successfully.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/lead_proposals.py uses lazy imports `from server import create_approval_task, ApprovalType, complete_approval_task, stamp_pdf_with_signature` inside handlers — correctly avoids circular imports at startup but means a typo / removal in server.py would only surface at request time. Consider extracting these helpers into their own module (e.g. services/approvals.py + services/pdf_signature.py) for cleaner dependencies once server.py is further dismantled.

FONTS dict in services/proposal_pdf.py registers each brand font in a single try/except per pair. If only the Bold TTF is missing/corrupt, the regular still gets registered then the dict update is skipped (the whole pair falls back to default base font). Minor robustness improvement: register regular + bold independently and gracefully fall back the bold to regular if needed.

ProposalShareEmailRequest validates `to_emails: List[EmailStr]` but does not enforce a non-empty list — calling /share-email with `to_emails=[]` on an approved proposal would attempt a Resend call with `to=[]` which fails late. Add `min_items=1` to to_emails.

share-email endpoint logs failures with the raw exception string in the 500 detail — this is OK for an internal CRM but could leak Resend API error context to the client. Consider returning a generic message and logging the full error server-side only.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — proposal section loads, Customize dialog opens, all 4 modern fonts visible in Title-font dropdown, Save persists Poppins

seed_data_creation: Created 4 throwaway templates named TEST_{font}_iter215 (one per modern font) — all deleted in test teardown. During frontend run, default template title.font was briefly set to 'poppins' via the UI and then restored to 'dejavu' via API PUT. No persistent test data remains. The test lead 08c93122-99fc-4587-b31c-559649f29c17 now has proposal version 29 (was 28 at start) — expected side-effect of test_generate_proposal + the final post-restore generate verification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 215 backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_215_lead_proposals.py — 10/10 green in 3.6s. To embed-check a modern font in a generated PDF: POST /api/proposals/templates {name}, PUT /api/proposals/templates/{id} with title.font + every section.heading_font/body_font set to the brand key, then POST /api/leads/{lead_id}/proposal/preview {template_id:<id>} and regex /BaseFont\s*/([A-Za-z0-9+\-]+) on response bytes — expect Poppins/Montserrat/Lato/RobotoSlab (with their -Bold variants). The test lead is 08c93122-99fc-4587-b31c-559649f29c17 (Drive Folder Test Lead). UI selectors: data-testid='tpl-title-card' (contains the Title font combobox as its FIRST role='combobox'), data-testid='save-template-btn', data-testid='apply-font-all-btn' (still works, sets all section fonts to title.font). The 4 new font option labels in the Title-font dropdown are: 'Poppins (modern)', 'Montserrat (modern)', 'Lato (modern)', 'Roboto Slab (serif)'.
rca of the issue: No bug to RCA — pure verification of a refactor (server.py → routes/lead_proposals.py) + a font addition (proposal_pdf.FONTS gained 4 brand TTFs in backend/assets/proposal/, FONTS array in ProposalTemplateSettings.js gained 4 entries). All paths unchanged, all existing frontend code paths keep working, and the new fonts embed correctly in generated PDFs.
